name: Demo Turnkey Font Ink Traps
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a neo-grotesque with geometric influences; ink traps are used both as a technical and decorative feature, consistent with contemporary neo-grotesque revivals.
summary: A neo-grotesque with oversized ink traps used as both a technical feature and a visual design element, available in 36 weights and styles.
description: Turnkey is a modern grotesque with oversized ink traps that function as a genuine design detail at larger sizes and a practical one in small body text. The family spans 36 styles across standard, italic, and soft variants, where the soft cuts introduce slightly rounded forms for a warmer feel. Each weight includes extended Latin and Cyrillic support covering over 90 languages, alongside fractions, tabular figures, arrows, ligatures and alternate glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Turnkey as a modern grotesque with oversized ink traps, positioned as a versatile typeface for both small and large sizes. They note the 36 weights and styles offer freedom to create, with each weight including support for over 90 languages, fractions, tabular figures, arrows, ligatures and alternate glyphs. A soft version of both the standard and italic is included, described as more friendly, warm and soft.

name: Barkanon
classification_notes: The foundry describes it explicitly as a humanist sans serif, blending modern styling with classic humanist flow; suitable for both display and text use.
summary: A humanist sans with nine weights and matching italics, designed to balance expressive display use with legibility in body copy.
description: Built on classic humanist proportions with a contemporary edge, Barkanon covers 96 languages across nine styles with full italic companions. Stylistic alternates, ligatures and additional OpenType features add flexibility for branding, editorial and identity work. The family occupies the middle ground between workhorse legibility and enough personality for headlines and logos.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Barkanon as going beyond a run-of-the-mill humanist sans serif, blending modern styling with classic humanist flow to create a bold, captivating look. Designed to be expressive where needed and legible throughout, it suits headings, body copy and logos, covering 96 languages, nine styles plus italics, with stylistic alternates, ligatures and other OpenType features, and includes web fonts.

name: Cribin Serif Display font
classification_notes: A high-contrast condensed serif designed explicitly for display use, drawing on vintage letterpress and grunge aesthetics rather than text setting traditions.
summary: A condensed, high-contrast display serif drawing on 90s grunge music and vintage letterpress traditions for use in logos, headlines and poster work.
description: Cribin combines strong serifs and pronounced stroke contrast in a condensed form suited to headlines, logotypes and editorial display work. The aesthetic references 90s grunge music culture and classic letterpress printing, giving it a rough-edged character distinct from conventional display serifs. Sold as a single style, it is positioned for branding, magazine covers and web or print projects where attitude and period flavour are the priority.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Cribin as a playful condensed serif that blends the rebellious vibe of 90s grunge music with vintage letterpress charm and the bold impact of classic posters. Its condensed form, strong serifs and high contrast are said to make every character a work of art, and the foundry recommend pairing it with CTCo Hopps.

name: Supertall Condensed
classification_notes: A tall, heavily condensed display face drawing on 80s video game and movie title aesthetics, designed purely for large-scale impact rather than text use.
summary: A tall, condensed display face that draws on 80s video game and movie title lettering for large-scale headline work.
description: Built for filling space at large sizes, it offers Regular and Bold weights each with standard obliques and reverse obliques, giving designers six styles in total. The reverse oblique option adds a distinctly retro, almost italicless feel suited to poster and title work. Inspiration runs from hard-hitting film titles to 8-bit game screens.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Supertall as a massive statement font designed to command a page. Taking cues from hard-hitting movie titles and 80s video game aesthetics, it combines that retro energy with a modern touch, and the family includes Regular and Bold weights alongside obliques and reverse obliques for added style.

name: CTCO Hopps Condensed
classification_notes: A condensed display grotesque drawn for headlines and posters, with vintage beer label and newspaper headline references placing it firmly in the display category rather than text use.
summary: A condensed display grotesque drawn from vintage beer labels and newspaper headlines, offered in regular and rounded cuts.
description: Four styles cover the family: Regular, Italic, Rounded and Rounded Italic, giving designers a choice between hard-edged and softened terminals within the same condensed structure. The vintage beer label and newspaper headline references lend it a period warmth that suits bold editorial work, poster typography and logo lockups. At this price point it sits as a functional, characterful display tool rather than a serious text companion.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe CTCO Hopps Condensed as a condensed display grotesque designed for headlines, posters and logos, inspired by vintage beer labels and newspaper headlines. It comes in four flavours: Regular, Rounded, Italic and Rounded Italic.

name: Something Rounded Blackletter Font
classification_notes: The foundry describes it as a serif blackletter with rounded forms, blending blackletter structure with serif details and a modern sensibility, making it a display-oriented blackletter rather than a purely historical revival.
summary: A rounded blackletter display font aimed at logo designers, blending serif detail with softened blackletter forms for headings and logotypes.
description: Designed with logo designers and typographers in mind, it combines blackletter structure with rounded, softened serifs and a vintage character. The font includes stylistic sets, discretionary ligatures and over 400 glyphs, covering Basic, Western European, Central European and South-Eastern European Latin scripts. Available as OTF and web fonts, it is positioned alongside a sharper companion face called Something New.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Something Rounded as a modern mix of serif and blackletter with a touch of vintage, suited to logotypes, heavy headings and coffee labels. It ships with stylistic sets, discretionary ligatures and coverage of Western, Central and South-Eastern European languages, with over 400 glyphs across OTF and web font formats.

name: Turnkey Font Ink Traps
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a modern grotesque with oversized ink traps and hints of geometric shapes, placing it squarely in the neo-grotesque tradition with a distinctive design detail.
summary: A neo-grotesque with oversized ink traps used as a deliberate design feature, available in two subfamilies covering standard and softly rounded styles across nine weights each.
description: The ink traps function both as ornamentation at display sizes and as a practical aid for small-text printing, giving the family a dual-purpose logic. Turnkey Soft introduces slight rounding to the standard and italic cuts, shifting the tone from corporate to warmer and more approachable. At 36 styles in total, with Cyrillic support and extended Latin covering 95 languages, it is built for broad deployment across editorial, identity and digital applications.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Turnkey as a modern grotesque with oversized ink traps and geometric hints, offering 36 weights and styles split between a standard set and a soft rounded variant. Ink traps serve as a design feature at larger sizes and a technical aid for small-body printing. Each weight includes extended language support covering over 90 languages including Cyrillic, alongside fractions, tabular figures, arrows, ligatures and alternate glyphs.

name: Gather Serif
classification_notes: Marketed as a modern classic serif with decorative ligatures and stylistic alternates, placing it in the contemporary display-leaning serif category.
summary: A contemporary serif with an extensive set of decorative ligatures and stylistic alternates aimed at editorial and branding use.
description: Gather Serif leans on its ligature set as its main selling point, covering over 30 combinations from CH and CO to ffl and ffi, alongside stylistic alternate glyphs. Latin language support covers Western European, Central European, South Eastern European, South American and Esperanto scripts. Positioned as an elegant display serif for branding and typographic work where ornamental detail is welcome.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Gather Serif as a modern classic, highlighting beautifully crafted letterforms and a rich selection of ligatures and stylistic alternates designed to bring a touch of elegance to typography.

name: Codo Mono
classification_notes: A fixed-width typeface with a modern, upright structure and no serif details, sitting squarely in the monospaced sans category suited to branding and editorial use.
summary: A modern monospaced typeface in six weights with matching italics and variable weight versions, covering Western, Central and South-Eastern European Latin.
description: Codo Mono offers six weights from Thin to Bold with full italic counterparts and variable weight versions for both upright and italic styles. Stylistic alternatives are included to add distinction in branding, packaging and editorial contexts. Latin script coverage extends across Western, Central and South-Eastern European languages.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Codo Mono as a carefully crafted monospaced typeface featuring stylistic alternatives to help designs and branding stand out, designed for great legibility with a modern feel and suited to branding, magazines, editorial copy and packaging.

name: Cake Bake
classification_notes: An all-caps hand-drawn bubble typeface with retro 1970s character; firmly in the display/novelty category with no text-setting application.
summary: A hand-drawn, all-caps bubble typeface with a 1970s retro character, sold in regular and outline cuts.
description: Drawn by hand for a natural, organic feel, this all-caps bubble face carries a distinctly 1970s flavour suited to logos, headings and short display copy. Alternate uppercase glyphs sit on the lowercase keys to introduce variation and avoid mechanical repetition. The pack includes both a filled and an outline version in OTF and web font formats.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Cake Bake as a fun bubble font full of character with 70s retro vibes, hand drawn for a natural flow and feel. It is all-uppercase with alternate glyphs on the lowercase keys, and ships with an outline variant included for good measure. Suggested uses include logos, headings, book titles and quotes wherever a cheeky, playful tone is needed.

name: Dead Meat
classification_notes: An all-caps, hand-drawn display typeface with deliberately irregular letterforms, placing it firmly in the display category with a hand-lettered subcategory.
summary: An all-caps, hand-drawn display font with deliberately irregular letterforms aimed at titles, logotypes and t-shirt graphics.
description: Each glyph is drawn from hand-lettered originals, giving the uppercase set an uneven, organic character across letters and numerals. Lowercase keystrokes trigger alternate characters, offering a degree of variation in setting. Coverage is limited to Western European Latin characters, supplied as OTF and WOFF.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Dead Meat as a bold, uppercase display font with a handmade lettering look, suited to titles, logotypes and t-shirt designs. Each glyph is unique, drawn from hand-lettered originals, and the pack includes all uppercase letters, numerals and a webfont format covering Western European characters.

name: Take Note
classification_notes: A casual handwritten display script with ligatures and stylistic alternates to simulate natural handwriting variation. The foundry categorises it as Craft Handwritten Display.
summary: A casual handwritten display script with ligatures and stylistic alternates designed for logotypes, blog headings and social media use.
description: A playful single-weight handwritten script that uses ligatures and stylistic alternates to produce a natural, flowing feel. Suited to logotypes, blog headings and short decorative text where informality and personality are wanted. Featured in the Typodarium 2022.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Take Note as a beautiful and playful handwritten font that uses ligatures and stylistic alternatives for a natural flow and feel, recommending it for logotypes, blog headings and Instagram quotes.

name: Bowler Hand Slab Serif
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a slab serif style with hand-drawn letterforms, giving it a rough, textured quality consistent with a hand-drawn slab subcategory.
summary: A hand-drawn slab serif created with a Rotring ink pen, offering a rough, vintage character across four weights.
description: Drawn by hand using a Rotring ink pen, this slab serif carries the natural irregularities and texture of analogue letterforms. Four weights from Light to Bold give enough range for display and editorial use where a vintage or craft aesthetic is wanted. The rough quality makes it better suited to short-form typesetting than extended body copy.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Bowler Hand as a font created from hand-drawn letterforms using a Rotring ink pen, characterised by a slab serif style that gives a rough, vintage feel. It comes in Light, Regular, Medium and Bold weights, supplied as OTF and webfonts.

name: Kin Grotesque Family
classification_notes: Self-described as a modern classic grotesque sans-serif, positioned for both body copy and display use across editorial and branding contexts.
summary: A nine-weight grotesque sans intended as a workhorse for editorial, body copy and logo work across Western and Central European scripts.
description: Offered in nine weights each with matching italics, it covers Western European, Central and South-eastern Latin character sets. The design sits in the modern classic grotesque tradition, broad enough in range to move between headline and body text settings. At £25 for the full family it pitches itself as an accessible general-purpose option for branding and editorial projects.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Kin Grotesque as a new go-to grotesque font built to perform across body copy and headlines alike. With nine standard weights and matching italics, they position it for editorial headings, body text and logo design, citing its personality as a fit for logos and brochures. Licensing beyond the standard desktop licence is available on request.

name: Adget Sans
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as inspired by classic geometric grotesque typefaces, with clean lines and large curves consistent with that genre.
summary: A geometric grotesque with ten weights and a variable font file, aimed at display and titling use.
description: Ten static weights plus a variable weight font make up this geometric grotesque, covering 328 glyphs with upper and lowercase, European characters, ligatures and stylistic alternates. The design draws on classic geometric grotesque conventions, favouring clean lines and generous curves for use in headings and portfolio work. A standard desktop licence is included at the base price.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Adget Sans as a modern, strong statement typeface with clean lines and big curves, inspired by classic geometric grotesque typefaces and suited to striking titles on portfolios and websites. The set includes ten individual weight fonts plus a variable weight font, with 328 glyphs covering upper and lowercase, numerals, European characters, ligatures and bonus stylistic alternates.

name: Something New Display Font
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes it as a serif blackletter, blending blackletter construction with serif details and a vintage character for display use.
summary: A serif blackletter display font in two styles, aimed at logo designers working on logotypes, heavy headings and packaging.
description: Blending blackletter structure with serif details and a vintage character, this is a display-focused family designed for logotypes, heavy headings and label work. It ships in Normal and Italic, with stylistic sets, discretionary ligatures and over 400 glyphs covering Basic, Western European, Central European and South-Eastern European Latin scripts. Available as OTF and web font formats.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Something New as a modern mix of serif and blackletter with a touch of vintage, built for logo designers and typographers. They position it as a feature-packed display font suited to logotypes, heavy headings and beer labels, with stylistic sets, discretionary ligatures and broad Latin language coverage.

name: Beloid Gothic
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a blackletter typeface with fraktur style, drawn with a 35-degree angled nib and geometric construction rooted in medieval script traditions.
summary: A geometric fraktur blackletter drawn with a 35-degree angled nib, aimed squarely at bold titling and logotype work.
description: Beloid Gothic takes fraktur letterforms and applies a geometric sensibility, using a 35-degree angled nib to give the strokes a consistent, structured rhythm. It covers a full European character set and is designed for display use at large sizes where the strong angles and medieval references read clearly. Best suited to logotypes, headlines and editorial contexts that want blackletter weight without historical pastiche.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Beloid Gothic as a modern blackletter typeface, strong and geometric in a fraktur style using a 35-degree angled nib. Taking inspiration from medieval scriptures, it was designed with bold titles and logotype in mind, and features a full European character set.

name: Brushy Brush Free Font
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a brush font, made using a brush technique; falls squarely into the brush script subcategory of script typefaces.
summary: A quickly made brush script offered free for personal and commercial use, with some post-session refinement.
description: Made in under an hour as an experiment, with a further hour or two spent tidying overlooked details. It is a casual, handmade brush script with the rough energy you would expect from a rapid exercise. Free for both personal and commercial use, though redistribution and resale are prohibited.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Brushy Brush as a brush font made in under an hour as an experiment, noting that while they achieved a usable result within that time, they spent an additional hour or two refining details that had been missed. It is offered completely free for personal and commercial use, with the request that users do not share or resell it.

name: Quickrest
classification_notes: A tall, quirky display face with deliberate irregularities and a handmade character, placing it firmly in the display category with a craft or vintage subcategory.
summary: A tall, irregular display family of 10 fonts with a handmade, vintage character across five weights and matching slanted versions.
description: Each character is drawn with deliberate individuality, giving the family an uneven, crafted quality suited to packaging, branding or editorial work in the handmade or vintage space. Five weights from Thin to Heavy are paired with slanted counterparts, covering a reasonable range of tonal contrast. The feel is casual and expressive rather than precise, which limits its use to contexts where that looseness is intentional.
foundry_blurb: Colt Type describe Quickrest as a tall and quirky family where no character conforms to the next, with its roots in the handmade, vintage and crafty scene. The set includes uppercase and lowercase letters, numerals, punctuation and ligatures across 10 fonts.
